config.description:
Gatus configuration — supplied as an external ConfigMap; this chart never
renders config. Set a Reloader annotation under `deploymentAnnotations`
(e.g. reloader.stakater.com/auto) if you want the pod to roll when the
ConfigMap changes.

existingConfigMap.description: REQUIRED: name of a ConfigMap holding your gatus config file(s) (templated, so a release-derived name works).
existingConfigMap.title: existingConfigMap

items.description: ConfigMap keys to mount read-only into gatus.configPath; each `{ key, path }` mounts the ConfigMap's `key` at `<configPath>/<path>` (subPath).
items.title: items

config.title: config

deploymentAnnotations.description: Annotations added to the Deployment (workload) metadata, e.g. `reloader.stakater.com/auto: "true"` so Stakater Reloader rolls the pod when the mounted config ConfigMap changes (Reloader reads the workload, not the pod).
deploymentAnnotations.title: deploymentAnnotations

gatus.description:
Upstream Gatus — image and runtime settings. This image is pulled, never built
by this repo.

configPath.description: Directory gatus reads (GATUS_CONFIG_PATH). The shared volume is mounted here; the sidecar writes its generated YAML here and the BYO ConfigMap files are overlaid on top. The chart always sets GATUS_CONFIG_PATH to this.
configPath.title: configPath

delayStartSeconds.description: Delay gatus startup by N seconds (GATUS_DELAY_START_SECONDS). Omitted when empty or 0.
delayStartSeconds.title: delayStartSeconds

env.description: Extra environment variables for the gatus container, as a map (templated), e.g. TZ or `${VAR}` substitution values referenced by your config.
env.title: env

envFrom.description:
- name: GOMEMLIMIT
    value: "200MiB"
Sources of environment variables for the gatus container (templated), e.g. a Secret holding buddy.yaml Pushover tokens.
envFrom.title: envFrom

extraEnv.description:
TZ: UTC
Extra environment variables for the gatus container, as a raw list (templated).
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

digest.description: Pin the gatus image by digest (sha256:…); when set, overrides the tag.
digest.title: digest

tag.description: Gatus image tag (Renovate-managed). Also becomes the chart appVersion at package time.
tag.title: tag

logLevel.description: Gatus log level (GATUS_LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR). Omitted when empty.
logLevel.title: logLevel

port.description: Container port, Service targetPort and probe port. Also exported as GATUS_WEB_PORT so your config can use `web.port: ${GATUS_WEB_PORT}`; gatus's actual listen port comes from its config file, so reference this var or match it.
port.title: port

securityContext.description:
- secretRef:
      name: gatus-secrets
Gatus container securityContext (no privilege escalation, read-only root filesystem, drops ALL capabilities).
securityContext.title: securityContext

startupProbe.description: Gatus startup probe (optional). Gates liveness/readiness while gatus starts; empty disables it.
startupProbe.title: startupProbe

gatus.title: gatus

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

httpRoute.description:
Gateway API HTTPRoute — an alternative to `ingress` for clusters using the
Gateway API. Renders one HTTPRoute pointing at the app service port. Named
`httpRoute` (not `route`) to avoid confusion with the OpenShift Route kind.

additionalRules.description:
- gatus.example.com
Custom rules prepended before the default rule (templated).
additionalRules.title: additionalRules

apiVersion.description: HTTPRoute apiVersion; empty defaults to gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1.
apiVersion.title: apiVersion

enabled.description: Expose the UI via a Gateway API HTTPRoute (alternative to ingress).
enabled.title: enabled

filters.description: Filters applied to the default rule.
filters.title: filters

hostnames.description:
- name: gateway
   namespace: gateway
   sectionName: https
Hostnames matched against the Host header (templated).
hostnames.title: hostnames

httpsRedirect.description: Redirect HTTP→HTTPS (301) instead of routing to the backend (needs HTTP+HTTPS listeners).
httpsRedirect.title: httpsRedirect

kind.description: HTTPRoute kind; empty defaults to HTTPRoute.
kind.title: kind

matches.description: Match conditions for the default rule.
matches.title: matches

parentRefs.description: Gateways (and listeners) this route attaches to.
parentRefs.title: parentRefs

httpRoute.title: httpRoute

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets for private registries.
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

enabled.description: Expose the UI via an Ingress.
enabled.title: enabled

initContainers.description: Extra initContainers (templated) added to the pod. They run, in order, before the gatus-sidecar native sidecar and the gatus container — use them to prep the shared config volume or wait on a dependency. For a long-running helper, set `restartPolicy: Always` to make it a native sidecar too.
initContainers.title: initContainers

enabled.description: Create a Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor (requires its CRDs). Scrapes the http port at `path`.
enabled.title: enabled

path.description: Metrics path (served on the gatus web port).
path.title: path

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in resource names.
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

enabled.description: Persist gatus.configPath (the sidecar's generated YAML and any sqlite DB) on a PVC. Disabled by default → the shared volume is an emptyDir and gatus runs in-memory (set memory storage in your config).
enabled.title: enabled

existingClaim.description: Use an existing PVC instead of creating one; when set, no PVC is rendered.
existingClaim.title: existingClaim

storageClass.description: StorageClass for the PVC; empty uses the cluster default.
storageClass.title: storageClass

podDisruptionBudget.description:
gatus is effectively single-instance (in-memory state, or a single RWO PVC when
persistence is on), so this guards only against *voluntary* disruption — node
drains, autoscaler scale-down.

enabled.description: Create a PodDisruptionBudget. ⚠️ With a single replica, the default `minAvailable: 1` makes a node drain block until you delete the pod yourself; set `maxUnavailable: 1` instead to let drains proceed. Off by default.
enabled.title: enabled

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum pods that may be unavailable, as a count or percentage; takes precedence over `minAvailable` when set.
maxUnavailable.title: maxUnavailable

minAvailable.description: Minimum pods that must stay available, as a count or percentage. Used unless `maxUnavailable` is set.
minAvailable.title: minAvailable

podDisruptionBudget.title: podDisruptionBudget

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level securityContext (runs as non-root uid/gid 65532 with fsGroup so the shared /config volume is writable).
podSecurityContext.title: podSecurityContext

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClass for the pod, so gatus is less likely to be preempted/evicted under node pressure. Empty uses the cluster default.
priorityClassName.title: priorityClassName

create.description: Create RBAC (a (Cluster)Role + binding) granting the sidecar the access it needs.
create.title: create

extraRules.description: Extra policy rules appended to the derived rules. The base rules are generated from the enabled `sidecar.kinds` (least privilege, get/list/watch), so you normally leave this empty.
extraRules.title: extraRules

type.description: RBAC scope: ClusterRole (watch all namespaces) or Role (single namespace; pair with the sidecar's `namespace`).
type.title: type

replicaCount.description: Number of gatus replicas. Gatus is backed by a Deployment; with persistence enabled (a single RWO PVC) keep this at 1.
replicaCount.title: replicaCount

resources.description: Pod-level resource requests/limits — the Kubernetes `Pod.spec.resources` field, one budget shared by the gatus and sidecar containers (no need to split it per container). Needs Kubernetes 1.34+, where PodLevelResources is beta and on by default; the chart's kubeVersion enforces this. Empty leaves it unset.
resources.title: resources

port.description: Service port (matches the gatus web/container port; /metrics is served here too).
port.title: port

automount.description: Automount the ServiceAccount API token (on by default: the sidecar needs Kubernetes API access to discover endpoints).
automount.title: automount

name.description: ServiceAccount name; generated from the release name if empty.
name.title: name

sidecar.description:
home-operations gatus-sidecar — a native sidecar that watches the cluster and
generates gatus endpoints.

annotationConfig.description: Annotation key for the per-resource YAML config override (--annotation-config); empty uses the sidecar default.
annotationConfig.title: annotationConfig

annotationEnabled.description: Annotation key for enabling/disabling per-resource processing (--annotation-enabled); empty uses the sidecar default.
annotationEnabled.title: annotationEnabled

defaultInterval.description: Default probe interval for generated endpoints (--default-interval).
defaultInterval.title: defaultInterval

enabled.description: Run the gatus-sidecar as a native sidecar (init container with restartPolicy: Always).
enabled.title: enabled

extraArgs.description: Extra raw flags appended to the sidecar args, e.g. `["--foo=bar"]`.
extraArgs.title: extraArgs

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables for the sidecar container, as a raw list (templated).
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

gatewayNames.description: Gateway name(s) to filter HTTPRoutes (--gateway-name, repeated per entry).
gatewayNames.title: gatewayNames

digest.description: Pin the sidecar image by digest (sha256:…); set by the release pipeline. When set, overrides the tag.
digest.title: digest

tag.description: Overrides the sidecar image tag; defaults to the chart version (the sidecar repo's own release). The release pipeline pins the digest instead.
tag.title: tag

ingressClasses.description: Ingress class(es) to filter Ingresses (--ingress-class, repeated per entry).
ingressClasses.title: ingressClasses

kinds.description: Per-kind discovery. `enable` turns the kind on; `auto` also auto-creates endpoints for matching resources; `prefix` prepends to generated endpoint names. RBAC rules are derived from whichever kinds are enabled. The default (httproute auto + service enable) mirrors the maintainer's real usage.
kinds.title: kinds

logLevel.description: Sidecar log level (--log-level: debug, info, warn, error).
logLevel.title: logLevel

namespace.description: Namespace to watch (--namespace); empty watches all namespaces (requires a ClusterRole).
namespace.title: namespace

output.description: File the sidecar writes generated YAML to (--output); empty defaults to `<gatus.configPath>/gatus-sidecar.yaml` (in the shared volume).
output.title: output

probePaths.description: Include paths from match rules in probe URLs (--probe-paths); false probes bare hostnames.
probePaths.title: probePaths

securityContext.description: gatus-sidecar container securityContext (no privilege escalation, read-only root filesystem, drops ALL capabilities).
securityContext.title: securityContext

sidecar.title: sidecar

strategy.description: Deployment update strategy. Defaults to `Recreate` because gatus can be stateful: with `persistence` on (a single ReadWriteOnce PVC) a RollingUpdate would start the new pod before the old one releases the volume, which a RWO volume can't satisfy — deadlocking the rollout. Set `{type: RollingUpdate, rollingUpdate: {...}}` for a stateless deployment (persistence off, or an RWX volume).
strategy.title: strategy

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Grace period for a clean shutdown (gatus drains in-flight checks and closes its servers on SIGTERM).
terminationGracePeriodSeconds.title: terminationGracePeriodSeconds

tests.description: Config for the chart's `helm test` hook (a pod that curls the Service's /health). Used only by `helm test`, never the workload.

repository.description: `helm test` connection-pod image; a gcr-mirrored curl, so the test never pulls from Docker Hub.
repository.title: repository

tests.title: tests

volumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts on the gatus container.
volumeMounts.title: volumeMounts

volumes.description: Additional volumes on the Deployment pod.
volumes.title: volumes
